---
title: "02 — Parse and Clean Articles"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas")
source("C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas/_config.R")
source("C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas/R/helpers.R")
```

## Load and clean article data

Read all Parquet files, add computed metadata columns, and write a
cleaned dataset partitioned by year.

```{r load-and-clean}
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)

# Open all article parquet files as a dataset
parquet_files <- list.files(DATA_PARQUET, pattern = "^articles_\\d{4}\\.parquet$",
                            full.names = TRUE)
message("Found ", length(parquet_files), " Parquet files")

# Process year by year to manage memory
cleaned_dir <- file.path(DATA_PARQUET, "articles_cleaned")
dir.create(cleaned_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (pf in parquet_files) {
  yr <- gsub(".*articles_(\\d{4})\\.parquet", "\\1", pf)
  outpath <- file.path(cleaned_dir, paste0("year=", yr), "part-0.parquet")

  if (file.exists(outpath)) {
    message("Year ", yr, " already cleaned, skipping.")
    next
  }

  message("Cleaning year ", yr, "...")
  df <- read_parquet(pf)

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    message("  Empty, skipping.")
    next
  }

  # Parse and add metadata
  dt <- as.data.table(df)

  # Parse date
  dt[, date := as.Date(date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")]
  dt[, year := as.integer(format(date, "%Y"))]
  dt[, month := as.integer(format(date, "%m"))]
  dt[, year_month := format(date, "%Y-%m")]

  # Parse page number
  dt[, page_num := parse_page(page)]
  dt[, front_page := as.integer(!is.na(page_num) & page_num == 1)]

  # Article metadata
  dt[, n_words := count_words(article)]
  dt[, headline_length := nchar(headline)]
  dt[, has_byline := as.integer(nchar(trimws(byline)) > 0)]

  # OCR quality proxy
  dt[, ocr_quality := 1 - share_nonalpha(article)]

  # Filter: require at least 10 words and reasonable OCR
  dt <- dt[!is.na(n_words) & n_words >= 10]

  # Ensure year is set for rows where date parsing failed
  dt[is.na(year), year := as.integer(yr)]

  # Write partitioned output
  dir.create(dirname(outpath), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  write_parquet(dt, outpath)
  message("  Saved ", nrow(dt), " cleaned articles for year ", yr)

  rm(df, dt)
  gc()
}
```

## Verify cleaned data

```{r verify}
cleaned_ds <- open_dataset(cleaned_dir, partitioning = "year")
message("Total cleaned articles: ",
        cleaned_ds |> count() |> collect() |> pull(n))
message("Columns: ", paste(names(cleaned_ds), collapse = ", "))

# Summary stats
summary_stats <- cleaned_ds |>
  summarise(
    n_articles = n(),
    n_newspapers = n_distinct(newspaper_name),
    min_date = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_date = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_words = mean(n_words, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_ocr = mean(ocr_quality, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  collect()

print(summary_stats)
```
